nutrition_note.from: Lean beef provides high‑quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates; lettuce and tomato add volume and micronutrients; cheddar contributes calcium and fat; salsa offers flavor with relatively low calories. To boost fiber, use whole‑grain tortillas or add beans; to reduce sodium, opt for low‑sodium salsa and lighter cheese.
nutrition_note.to: Birria tacos provide substantial protein from slow-cooked beef and are flavorful due to concentrated cooking liquids. Because they're often seared in oil and served with rich consommé, they can be higher in fat and sodium. To lighten, trim visible fat from the meat before cooking, strain and skim excess fat from the consommé, use modest oil for searing, and serve with extra vegetables or a side salad to add fiber and volume.

short_description.from: A quick, customizable taco night featuring lean beef, corn tortillas, fresh veggies, cheddar, and salsa.
short_description.to: Flavorful slow-simmered beef birria served in warm corn tortillas, dipped in the rich consommé and topped with cilantro, onion, and a squeeze of lime — a hearty, savory taco night favorite.

to.name: optional: queso fresco or Oaxaca cheese

meal_suggestion.from: Assemble made‑to‑order tacos: warm corn tortillas, top with lean beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, cheddar, and salsa. Optional lime, avocado, or light sour cream. Pair with a simple side salad or beans for extra fiber.
meal_suggestion.to: Prepare a pot of beef birria by slow-cooking chuck roast (or short ribs) with dried chiles (guajillo, ancho), tomatoes, garlic, onion, cumin, oregano, and bay leaves until very tender. Shred the meat and keep the cooking liquid (consommé) warm. To assemble, lightly dip corn tortillas in the consommé and sear on a hot griddle until slightly crisp. Fill with shredded birria, chopped onion, cilantro, and a squeeze of lime. Serve with small bowls of consommé for dipping on the side and optional pickled onions or sliced radishes.

updates.reasoning: Transformed the Father's taco meal from a maple-kissed DIY skillet concept into Birria Tacos. Rewrote name, description, meal suggestion, ingredients, and nutrition note to reflect the new dish.
